question: A company tracks average website sign-ups per year. Based on the chart, which statement is most appropriate?

options.A: The latest year shows a sharp decline compared to previous years.
options.B: The latest year proves that sign-ups are now permanently declining.
options.C: The latest year has the highest sign-ups in the entire period.
options.D: The chart only shows sign-ups up to early 2024, so it does not justify conclusions about the full-year trend.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 2 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (301 sign-ups) directly to full-year values like 1,685 sign-ups creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
